task_completion.description: Verify that the input line layer is loaded into QGIS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QGIS Layers panel shows the 'sample_lines' layer.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Points along geometry' tool is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Points along geometry' dialog window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the tool is configured with the correct distance and offsets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Points along geometry' dialog, the 'Distance' parameter is set to 0.05, and both 'Start offset' and 'End offset' are set to 0.

task_completion.description: Verify that the points are successfully generated along the lines.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new points layer (e.g., 'Interpolated points') appears in the Layers panel, and multiple points are visible along the lines in the map canvas.

task_completion.description: Verify that the generated points are being exported to the correct file path and format with the correct CRS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Vector Layer as...' dialog is visible, with Format set to 'GeoJSON', File name set to '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/inspection_points.geojson', and CRS set to EPSG:4326.

task_completion.description: Verify that the export process completed successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message banner (e.g., 'Layer exported successfully') is visible, or the new 'inspection_points' layer appears in the Layers panel.

integrity.description: The agent used the QGIS GUI to perform the task rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with QGIS menus, toolbars, and dialogs (like 'Points along geometry' and 'Save Vector Layer as').

integrity.description: The agent actually ran the geoprocessing tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progress bar or log in the tool dialog, or the appearance of the generated points layer in the map canvas.

integrity.description: The agent did not just create a text file with the expected name and content using a text editor or terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The absence of terminal windows or text editors being used to write the GeoJSON file directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input file contains 2 LineString features representing roads in WGS84 (EPSG:4326). Using the QGIS 'Points along geometry' tool with a 0.05 degree spacing parameter, approximately 17 points are expected to be generated (10 from Road 1 and 7 from Road 2). The expected output is a GeoJSON file at /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/inspection_points.geojson with Point geometry features in EPSG:4326.
